title: mussaenda glabra var. wallichii
intro: Mussaenda glabra var. wallichii, also known as the Indian cork tree or Malay tea tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. Its health benefits include treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as having antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. The leaves and flowers of the plant are used to prepare herbal infusions, teas, and infusions for medicinal purposes.
